skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI video creation agent that produces short videos (5-120 seconds) including product ads, social media content, brand videos, and explainers. Handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music automatically.
skill.summary: Professional AI video production skill that creates short-form videos for marketing, social media, and brand content with automated creative workflows.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 534 patterns across 13 files. Most findings are FALSE POSITIVES: shell command substitution patterns are standard bash scripting, hidden file access targets ~/.pexo/config for credential storage (documented requirement), and network URLs point to the legitimate pexo.ai service. The skill is a transparent API client with no evidence of malicious behavior, data exfiltration, or hidden functionality. Risk is elevated to 'low' (not 'safe') due to shell script execution requiring user trust.

low_findings.title: Shell Script Execution Pattern
low_findings.description: Multiple bash scripts use command substitution and source external configuration files. This is standard CLI tooling but requires user trust in script contents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Scripts use standard bash patterns (#!/usr/bin/env bash, source, $(...)) documented as CLI tool behavior - not obfuscated or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Configuration File Access
low_findings.description: Scripts read credentials from ~/.pexo/config file. This is documented and expected for API client tools, but represents credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Configuration file path is explicitly documented in SKILL.md lines 30-35 with clear purpose for API key storage.

low_findings.title: API Key Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Scripts require PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL environment variables. These are declared requirements in the skill metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable requirements are explicitly declared in SKILL.md lines 12-14 under 'requires.env' section.

low_findings.title: External API Communication
low_findings.description: Scripts make HTTP requests to pexo.ai domain. URLs are hardcoded and point to the legitimate service provider.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network destinations (pexo.ai, pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com) are documented as the official Pexo service endpoints.

content.value_statement: Transform text prompts and uploaded media into polished short-form videos without manual editing. Pexo handles scriptwriting, composition, transitions, and music automatically while you focus on creative direction.
actual_capabilities: Generate 5-120 second videos from text prompts with automatic scriptwriting and shot composition
Process uploaded images, videos, and audio files as source material for video creation
Support multiple aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) for different platform requirements
Provide preview options for user selection before final render
Handle iterative revisions through conversational feedback
Manage project state and polling for video completion status
limitations: Cannot crawl external URLs - users must download and upload files manually
Video production requires 15-20 minutes processing time for short videos
Requires valid Pexo API key and active credits balance
Asset tags must wrap uploaded file IDs for proper reference in prompts

use_cases.title: Marketing Professional Creating Product Ads
use_cases.description: Upload product photos and generate promotional videos for social media campaigns, e-commerce listings, or digital advertising with minimal creative effort.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers and small business owners

use_cases.title: Content Creator Producing Social Media Clips
use_cases.description: Transform ideas into engaging TikTok, Instagram Reels, or YouTube Shorts content by describing the desired video and letting AI handle production.
use_cases.target_user: Social media influencers and content creators

use_cases.title: Business Team Building Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Create professional explainer videos for product demos, training materials, or customer onboarding without hiring video production agencies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product showcase video for running shoes. Show the shoes from multiple angles with dynamic transitions and upbeat music.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple product video

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Ad with Uploaded Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a vertical 9:16 Instagram Reel promoting my coffee shop. Use the uploaded photo as the main visual, add text overlays about our morning special, and include energetic background music.
prompt_templates.scenario: User providing custom imagery for branded content

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Video Revision
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but can you make it more vibrant? Increase the color saturation and add smoother transitions between scenes. Also change the music to something more relaxing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Refining an existing video based on feedback

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Asset Brand Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand story video using these three uploaded images: our logo, team photo, and office exterior. Add a professional narrative about our company values and end with our tagline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating comprehensive brand content

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second video showing a sunset over mountains with calming music
output: Final deliverable: 15-second MP4 video (1080p, 16:9) with AI-generated sunset scene and royalty-free ambient music track

output_examples.input: Make a product video using uploaded shoe photo with text overlay saying 'Summer Sale'
output: Preview available - choose preferred style A or B
Final deliverable: 30-second promotional video with rotating product shots, animated 'Summer Sale' text overlay, and upbeat background music

best_practices: Provide specific details about video style, duration preferences, and target platform to guide the AI toward your vision
Upload high-quality source images and videos for better output quality - the AI enhances but cannot fix poor source material
Use the revision workflow for refinements rather than creating new projects - this preserves context and reduces costs
Allow 15-20 minutes for video generation before checking status - frequent polling does not speed up production
anti_patterns: Do not add creative direction the user did not request - relay their exact words to preserve their creative intent
Avoid calling chat scripts during WAIT status - this causes duplicate production jobs and wasted credits
Do not send truncated video URLs to users - include full URL with all query parameters or downloads will fail
Never skip the asset tag wrapping step - bare asset IDs in messages are ignored by the Pexo backend

faq.question: How long does video creation take?
faq.answer: A 15-second video typically takes 15-20 minutes to generate. Longer or more complex videos may require additional time. The skill polls automatically and notifies you when complete.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, TIFF, HEIC. Videos: MP4, MOV, AVI. Audio: MP3, WAV, AAC, M4A, OGG, FLAC. Files are uploaded via pexo-upload.sh and referenced with asset tags.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The skill detects credit errors automatically and displays purchase instructions. Visit https://pexo.ai/home, click Credits (top-right), then Buy Credits to add more.

faq.question: Can I modify a video after it's created?
faq.answer: Yes, use the same project_id and send revision feedback via pexo-chat.sh. Describe what you want changed and the AI will regenerate accordingly.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Three aspect ratios: 16:9 (widescreen for YouTube), 9:16 (vertical for TikTok/Reels), and 1:1 (square for Instagram posts). Specify your preference in the initial prompt.

faq.question: Why can't I send a URL instead of uploading a file?
faq.answer: Pexo cannot crawl external URLs. Download the file to your local system first, then upload it using pexo-upload.sh. This ensures reliable file access during video generation.
